title: koyamacalia phyllolepis
intro: Koyamacalia phyllolepis, also known as Koyama's golden ragwort, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Japan. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and fever, as well as treat various infections. Herbal preparations of Koyamacalia phyllolepis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are believed to offer various health benefits, including immune system support and antioxidant effects.
